2605.23448.title: AI Security Research Should Better Incentivize Defense Research
2605.23448.paper_summary: This work examines an imbalance in artificial intelligence (AI) security research: the field tends to produce more work on attacking AI systems than on defending them. Drawing on related academic papers, we find biased attack-to-defense ratios across subfields, including federated learning, speech recognition, membership inference, large language models, etc. The imbalance possibly means far beyond a simple count: attack papers are routinely evaluated under favorable conditions that make threats look more severe than they are in practice, while defenses are held to a stricter standard that few can meet. The result is a literature rich in demonstrated vulnerabilities and thin on usable and deployed protections. We thus argue that AI security research should better incentivize defense research.
2605.23448.paper_summary_zh: 這項工作探討了人工智慧（AI）安全研究中的一種不平衡：該領域傾向於產出更多有關攻擊AI系統的研究，而不是防禦它們的研究。<lb/>根據相關的學術論文，我們發現各子領域之間的攻擊與防禦比率存在偏差，包括聯邦學習、語音識別、成員推斷、大型語言模型等。<lb/>這種不平衡可能意味著遠不止簡單的計數：攻擊論文通常在有利的條件下進行評估，使威脅看起來比實際上更為嚴重，而防禦則被要求達到更嚴格的標準，這是很少有人能夠達到的。<lb/>因此，結果是文獻中充滿了已證明的漏洞，但可用和已部署的防護措施卻很薄弱。<lb/>因此，我們主張AI安全研究應該更好地激勵防禦研究。
2605.23448.author: Youqian Zhang et.al.
